findings.problem: The prose repeatedly uses the same hinge-sentence template to announce emotional turns (for example, "That was..." and "There it was..."). Because these pivots recur at key beats, the chapter starts labeling its shifts instead of discovering them in fresh syntax, which flattens otherwise strong scene pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the pivot sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:329, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:355 so the turns land through varied sentence shapes tied to Halima's perception, action, or a local image rather than the repeated summary hinge. Keep the same narrative beats, but make at least half of those pivots resolve through concrete sensory or bodily detail instead of a declarative label.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:329, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:355, no more than two emotional-pivot sentences rely on a "That/There/It was" summary hinge, and each revised beat can be distinguished by a different syntactic move or sensory trigger when read in sequence.

summary: The chapter cleanly lands its required spine beats, keeps Halima and Nura in voice against the style bible, and sustains believable dialogue pressure, but the prose does not reach pass level because too many emotional turns are framed through the same hinge-sentence construction.
